does: This paper introduces DecodingTrust-Agent Platform (DTap), a comprehensive red-teaming platform designed to systematically evaluate the security and safety of AI agents operating in real-world scenarios. The platform spans 14 distinct domains with over 50 simulation environments replicating widely-deployed systems including Google Workspace, PayPal, and Slack. DTap provides controllable and interactive environments where researchers can test how adversaries might manipulate agents into performing harmful actions such as leaking API keys, deleting user data, or executing unauthorized transactions. The platform enables large-scale risk assessment and reproducible evaluation of agent vulnerabilities in dynamic, untrusted environments involving external tools, heterogeneous data sources, and user interactions that characterize deployed AI agent systems.
connects: This work addresses critical gaps in AI agent safety evaluation that have emerged as autonomous agents become increasingly deployed for complex, high-stakes workflows. It builds on prior research in adversarial testing and red-teaming methodologies originally developed for language models and other AI systems, adapting these techniques to the unique challenges of agent-based architectures. The paper responds to growing concerns about agent manipulation demonstrated in real-world incidents and extends existing safety evaluation frameworks to handle the additional complexity introduced by agent-environment interactions, tool use, and decision-making across multiple steps. DTap synthesizes insights from both AI safety and security testing communities to create infrastructure for systematic vulnerability assessment.
enables: The platform enables future research into agent robustness, adversarial mitigation strategies, and defense mechanisms against manipulation attacks. By providing standardized, reproducible evaluation environments, DTap supports the development and benchmarking of more secure agent architectures and decision-making processes. The work facilitates large-scale empirical studies of agent vulnerabilities across diverse domains, enabling researchers to identify common failure modes and develop generalizable safety interventions. Furthermore, DTap creates infrastructure for establishing community standards in agent security testing and supports the development of trustworthy AI agent deployment practices across industry applications requiring high-stakes decision-making.
